source: topper64.co.uk/nk/btd6/rounds (health curve + superceramic) + bloons.fandom.com Late Game and Freeplay (BTD6) / Big Airship of Doom / Round 140 (CC BY-NC-SA / community)
scaling_source: Round-relative bloon scaling is NOT in the BTD Mod Helper game data dump: round files store only bloon composition + spawn timing, and BloonModel stores only the BASE maxHealth (BAD = 20000). BTD6 applies a RUNTIME late-game/freeplay health ramp to MOAB-class bloons (MOAB/BFB/ZOMG/DDT/BAD): from round 81 a piecewise-linear multiplier v(r) = base + (r - anchor_round) * per_round on the bracket whose min_round <= r <= max_round (rounds below the first bracket are unscaled = 1.0). The curve is VALIDATED against two independent anchors: v(100)=1.40 (BAD 20000 -> 28000 HP, 67200 RBE) and v(140)=5.00 (fortified BAD 40000 -> 200000 HP). It steepens sharply past round 100 (NOT a flat +2%/round) and reaches its max around round 500. Regular (non-MOAB-class) bloons do NOT take this multiplier; instead, from round 81 every Ceramic Bloon is replaced by a Super Ceramic (60 HP shell, 120 fortified) with only a single halved line of children -> freeplay RBE 68 (128 fortified), vs base ceramic 104. Round-scaled RBE is the base spawn tree recomputed with both rules; for any MOAB-class bloon the tree is just {MOAB-class bodies x v(r)} + {ceramic leaves -> superceramic}. Standard RBE is validated exact at r100 (BAD = 67200); fortified RBE is model-derived (~0.1% vs the wiki's r140 fortified figure, likely wiki rounding).
